title: Ugni stenophylla
taxonomy: Ugni stenophylla is classified within the family Myrtaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family includes many species of shrubs and trees found in tropical and subtropical regions. Ugni stenophylla is native to the temperate rainforests of South America, where it grows as an evergreen shrub. The classification of Ugni stenophylla is further divided into the genus Ugni, which is a small group of plants native to the southern hemisphere. The genus Ugni is closely related to the genus Syzygium, which includes many species of trees and shrubs commonly known as the "water apple".

intro: Ugni stenophylla, also known as Chilean guava or queñua, is a small, flowering evergreen shrub native to the temperate regions of South America, particularly in Chile and Argentina. The plant is valued for its edible fruits and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ugni stenophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ugni stenophylla is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the production of jams, preserves, and other culinary products due to its sweet and slightly tart flavor.
